title: verilog-ethernet ARP retry counter integer overflow
hook_30s: This ARP block retries a lookup until a 16-bit retry limit, then gives up. But the counter is only 8 bits. Set a limit above 255 and the counter wraps back to zero before it ever reaches the limit — so it never gives up, and the request retries forever. ChipGPT proves the overflow formally and in simulation.

steps.agent: I'll analyze this RTL against the spec systematically.
Reading the spec; extracting the canonicality rule.
Spec rule on line 8: counter MUST represent every value up to retry_limit. RTL `retry_q` is `[7:0]` (line 13) but `retry_limit_i` is `[15:0]`. Let me locate the comparison and counter logic.

retry_q is declared logic [7:0] (arp.sv:13) and incremented by 8'd1, but it is compared against the 16-bit input retry_limit_i in give_up_o = (retry_q >= retry_limit_i). For any retry_limit_i > 255 the 8-bit counter wraps 255->0 before it can ever reach the limit, so the >= comparison is never satisfied.

artifact.spec_section: Retry counting — "The retry counter MUST be able to represent every value up to retry_limit" (spec line 8)
artifact.explanation: The spec requires give_up to become satisfiable once the number of issued retries reaches retry_limit. With retry_limit_i=300 the harness drives requests until req_count>=300 and asserts give_up_o must be high. On the buggy 8-bit RTL the assertion fails because retry_q wrapped and never reaches 300.
artifact.result: SymbiYosys (z3): counterexample found — bug proven formally

steps.agent: Running the directed test in the simulator.

artifact.name: arp_retry_overflow_limit300
artifact.stimulus: Reset, set retry_limit_i=16'd300, then pulse req_sent_i for 600 ARP requests (>2x the limit); check give_up_o.
artifact.result_on_buggy: FAIL — give_up_o never asserts after 600 requests; 8-bit retry_q wrapped 255->0, comparison never true.
artifact.result_on_fixed: PASS — with 16-bit retry_q the counter reaches 300 and give_up_o asserts.

artifact.title: ARP retry counter (8-bit) too narrow for 16-bit retry_limit_i — give_up_o never asserts for limits > 255 (CWE-190)
artifact.category: Integer overflow / counter width mismatch (spec-to-RTL drift)
artifact.root_cause: retry_q is declared logic [7:0] (arp.sv:13) and incremented by 8'd1, but it is compared against the 16-bit input retry_limit_i in give_up_o = (retry_q >= retry_limit_i). For any retry_limit_i > 255 the 8-bit counter wraps 255->0 before it can ever reach the limit, so the >= comparison is never satisfied.
artifact.fix: Widen the counter to match the limit field: declare 'logic [15:0] retry_q;' and increment with 16'd1 (per PR #75). The comparison retry_q >= retry_limit_i then covers the full limit range.
artifact.impact: ARP lookups with retry_limit_i > 255 never reach the 'give up' condition; give_up_o stays low forever and the request retransmits indefinitely. The lookup hangs, leaking bandwidth and never failing over — a silent denial-of-service / liveness defect that only manifests for large configured limits.

artifact.test: arp_retry_overflow_limit300 (sim FAIL on buggy) + formal BMC depth 320 (CEX)
artifact.coverage_status: Covered — proven both formally (CEX) and by directed simulation (FAIL); fix verified by inspection per PR #75

proof.note: Arithmetic width bug — formal finds a reachable limit where give_up never asserts; a directed test drives 300 retries and watches give_up stay low.
